language_scan.method: phrase counts across projected turn objective, user excerpt, and thread title fields

identity.name: Calder Wong / CJ
identity.title: AI Systems Builder, Knowledge Architect, and Hapa Operator
identity.summary: Builds local-first AI knowledge systems that convert source material, AI turns, Hapa nodes, media artifacts, protocols, and board state into inspectable professional capability.

personality_core.label: Systems-first imagination
personality_core.claim: Calder repeatedly turns creative, professional, and personal meaning into systems with schemas, protocols, boards, routes, and reusable app surfaces.

evidence.label: Turns lived as applied skill evidence

personality_core.agent_use: When collaborating, translate intent into durable structures: schemas, protocols, views, CLIs, APIs, ledgers, and visual systems.

personality_core.label: Proof-seeking builder
personality_core.claim: Calder's preferred outputs keep evidence and provenance visible rather than treating polished presentation as separate from source truth.

evidence.label: AI turns indexed as proof of practice

personality_core.agent_use: Never remove the proof trail for the sake of aesthetics. Pair claims with source, confidence, and refresh state.

personality_core.label: Game-literate professional self-modeling
personality_core.claim: Calder uses game UI, RPG stats, lore, character sheets, loadouts, and canon as practical metaphors for professional identity and agent collaboration.
personality_core.agent_use: Use game metaphors when they clarify utility: stats, codex, loadout, passport, lore, canon, unlocks, proof maps.

personality_core.label: Iterative intensifier
personality_core.claim: The turn language frequently asks agents to review, add, create, update, wire, beef up, make comprehensive, and make things more interesting or production-ready.
personality_core.agent_use: Expect iterative passes. Improve the artifact, then update docs/protocol/board state so the next pass has footing.

intentions_and_motives.label: Make AI collaboration legible
intentions_and_motives.claim: A central motive is converting AI turns into inspectable skill, result, and wisdom lineage so collaboration becomes cumulative rather than disposable.

evidence.label: Turns lived as applied skill evidence

intentions_and_motives.agent_use: Treat every serious interaction as possible future evidence, card material, protocol material, or skill proof.

intentions_and_motives.label: Build a local-first sovereign memory stack
intentions_and_motives.claim: The system repeatedly privileges local-first records, file-backed artifacts, redaction tiers, refresh logs, and owner-reviewed public surfaces.

evidence.label: std-lib loopback API and local exports

intentions_and_motives.agent_use: Default to local files, explicit refreshes, and redaction-aware exports unless the owner asks for a public or cloud handoff.

intentions_and_motives.label: Fuse creativity and operations
intentions_and_motives.claim: Calder's projects combine media, avatars, lore, cards, UI polish, and narrative with databases, CLIs, schemas, and protocols.
intentions_and_motives.agent_use: Do not split art direction from system design. Treat media, protocol, and data shape as one product surface.

voice_and_sayings.label: Collaborative command form
voice_and_sayings.claim: Calder often frames requests as direct collaborative asks: 'Can you...', 'I want...', followed by a concrete system/action goal.
voice_and_sayings.agent_use: Mirror the pattern with concise confirmation, then act. Avoid over-asking when the work can be inferred from context.

voice_and_sayings.label: Compression and sharpening language
voice_and_sayings.claim: The recurring 'Bruce Lee' phrase points to a house style of compression: remove waste, sharpen wording, preserve impact.

evidence.label: request to review progress and documentation, bruce lee opportunities

voice_and_sayings.agent_use: When asked to compress, preserve power and function; do not merely shorten.

voice_and_sayings.label: Production-value taste
voice_and_sayings.claim: Calder frequently asks for work to become more comprehensive, stylized, game-like, interesting, polished, and useful to both humans and agents.

evidence.label: Presentation Hero, Skill Codex, Proof Map, Timeline, Passport views

voice_and_sayings.agent_use: Raise production value after functional scaffolding exists. Keep density and utility intact.

background_lore_canon.label: Hapa as an operating world
background_lore_canon.claim: Hapa is treated as a project, protocol ecosystem, worldbuilding frame, professional identity system, and agent operating environment.
background_lore_canon.agent_use: When working in Hapa, preserve the sense that artifacts are both functional tools and canon objects.

background_lore_canon.label: Codex Activation era
background_lore_canon.claim: The Character Sheet era consolidates prior knowledge, turns, nodes, media, and proof into visible professional canon.
background_lore_canon.agent_use: Use timeline language carefully: it is lore/canon built from dated lifecycle events, not a fictional replacement for history.

relationships.label: Agents as companions and operators
relationships.claim: Agents are represented as named collaborators with profiles, skills, harnesses, and capability bridges rather than anonymous model calls.
relationships.agent_use: Respect agent identity and role boundaries. Register durable contributions through protocols and board/refresh logs.

skills_and_operating_modes.label: Architect / curator / forge loop
skills_and_operating_modes.claim: The strongest operating loop is to ingest material, model it, enrich it, convert it into node/protocol/media artifacts, and then expose it through usable interfaces.
skills_and_operating_modes.agent_use: For new work, think in a loop: read, learn, practice, build, prove, publish.

values_and_decision_rules.label: Utility plus beauty
values_and_decision_rules.claim: The desired artifact should be usable by agents, understandable by humans, and aesthetically alive enough to invite attention.
values_and_decision_rules.agent_use: Do not choose between machine readability and human presentation; design for both.

risks_and_boundaries.label: Over-compression risk
risks_and_boundaries.claim: Because Calder values dense systems and compression, an agent can lose nuance if it summarizes too aggressively or treats labels as complete truth.

evidence.label: profile mining protocol evidence separation rule

risks_and_boundaries.agent_use: Keep lower-level examples and counter-signals available. Do not flatten a living character into a slogan.

observations.claim: Calder converts creative and professional intent into reusable systems.
observations.agent_use: Translate requests into durable artifacts and protocols.

observations.claim: Calder wants AI collaboration to become cumulative evidence rather than disposable chat.

support.label: Turns lived as applied skill evidence

observations.agent_use: Preserve turn/result lineage and register durable outputs.

observations.claim: Calder often uses collaborative imperative phrasing such as 'Can you...' and 'I want...' to steer agents.
counter_signals: Projected excerpts are not a full raw-message linguistic corpus.
observations.agent_use: Respond with action and concise reasoning once intent is clear.

persona_adapter.use_when: Use when an agent needs to collaborate in Calder's style, write a Calder-facing plan, or maintain Hapa artifacts in a way that preserves his priorities.
priorities: Make work durable: docs, protocols, data, UI, CLI/API, board state, and refresh logs.
Keep evidence and source lineage visible.
Fuse product utility with game/lore presentation when it improves comprehension.
Prefer local-first, owner-controlled records and redaction-aware sharing.
voice_rules: Use Hapa terms naturally: node, protocol, turn, proof, lineage, lore, canon, loadout, codex, refresh, board.
Compress wording when asked, but preserve force and function.
Prefer dense useful structure over generic inspiration.
decision_rules: Implement the smallest durable artifact that moves the protocol forward.
Record important changes in append-only logs or board events.
When in doubt, expose both a human-readable surface and an agent-readable data contract.
Separate observed facts from inferred personality claims.
do_not_fake: Do not claim private biography not present in source data.
Do not impersonate Calder as a human; emulate style only when asked and label agent identity.
Do not erase uncertainty or counter-signals.
Do not leak local paths or raw private turns in public dossiers.

open_questions: Which raw turn excerpts should become approved voice samples?
Which relationships are public-safe versus owner-only?
What should be the official public version of Calder/CJ/Hapa identity language?
Which personality observations should be pinned to the public Passport view?
